description:
# Open question

`bl sync` and `bl prime` walkthroughs — ops with hook dirs like any other, not tracker-exclusive.

Settled framing (per §6/§7/§8):
- Each is an op with `sync/pre|post` and `prime/pre|post` run-parts dirs; every linked plugin runs, cwd = operating/ (no change worktree — there is no ball-file diff to stage). No stdin return channel; plugins act via the filesystem (§7).
- Failure → reverse-order rollback, same as any op.

Resolve the remaining shape:
- `bl sync`: tracker fetches and fast-forwards, or aborts on contention ("remote wins, user re-runs"). Other plugins may refresh caches. Is there a `sync/pre` contention probe (a fast "remote has moved" check) before the work, or just a single `sync`?
- `bl prime`: comprehensive setup/refresh — tracker syncs, other plugins bootstrap/fetch external state. Relationship to `sync`: does prime call sync, or are they peers?
- What context does a sync/prime plugin get, given there is no `command`/`previous_state` payload and no change worktree? Define what is on the wire / in env for these ops (cwd = operating/, BALLS_* env, the §5 trailer metadata of the triggering op).
title: Topic: bl sync and bl prime walkthroughs
